title: Equity Buyback Agreement
description: Governs the company's repurchase of vested or unvested founder/employee shares upon departure.
whatsCovered: Purchase price calculation for vested shares
Automatic return of unvested stock options
Release of claims against the company by shareholder
Tax reporting of stock repurchase events
whenToUse: Use this contract to execute the startup's right to repurchase shares from a departing founder or executive to clean up the cap table.

fields.label: Company Office Address
fields.placeholder: e.g. 100 Innovators Blvd, San Francisco, CA

clauses.heading: EQUITY BUYBACK AGREEMENT
clauses.body: This startup agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into as of {effective_date} (the "Effective Date"), by and between {company_name}, located at {company_address} (the "Company"), and the counterparty or stakeholders executing this document below.

clauses.heading: 1. Core Agreement Terms
clauses.body: This agreement sets out the transaction and operational terms for Equity Buyback Agreement. The parties agree to coordinate in good faith to complete milestones, share resources, or grant rights as specified.

clauses.heading: 2. Repurchase Covenants
clauses.body: Company shall buy back {shares_repurchased} shares from Departing Shareholder {departing_holder} for the total repurchase price of {repurchase_price}.

clauses.heading: 3. Proprietary Information and Secrecy
clauses.body: Both parties agree to treat all financial metrics, corporate plans, cap tables, and research files received during the execution of this Agreement as confidential and proprietary.

clauses.heading: 4. Governing Law
clauses.body: This Agreement shall be governed and interpreted under the laws of the State/Country of {governing_state}.

faq.question: Why do startups have buyback agreements?
faq.answer: To prevent departed founders or employees from holding onto large, voting equity stakes while no longer contributing to the company's growth.

faq.question: At what price does the company buy back unvested stock?
faq.answer: Unvested stock is typically bought back at the lower of its original purchase price or its current fair market value.
